item.title: Love Island star tells followers 'you are loved' as he shares mental health challenges
item.description: Alex George said he wanted to help reduce "shame and stigma" around mental health challenges.

item.title: Man shot by police was 'harmless', say neighbours
item.description: As an investigation into the shooting begins, residents say the incident has left them "numb".

item.title: Drug addiction counsellor sentenced in Matthew Perry's overdose death
item.description: The drug counsellor was one of five people accused of supplying ketamine to Perry and exploiting his drug addiction.

item.title: French national shows symptoms on return from hantavirus-hit ship
item.description: Five passengers of the MV Hondius will be quarantined in Paris "until further notice", France's prime minister says.

item.title: The key measures in the King's Speech
item.description: The monarch has outlined the government's plans for new laws in the next session of Parliament.

item.title: UK alcohol deaths fall for first time since Covid pandemic
item.description: Experts say the “modest reduction” is “not cause for complacency”, with calls to redouble efforts to slash deaths further.

item.title: Brother feared Nottingham triple killer would take his own life
item.description: Valdo Calocane's brother, Elias, told a public inquiry he felt "powerless" when it came to his illness.

item.title: Nigerian film star Alexx Ekubo dies aged 40
item.description: The actor was recognised for his contributions to entertainment as well as his humanitarian efforts.

item.title: White-tailed eagles to be released in Exmoor despite farmer warnings
item.description: Some farmers fear the reintroduction of the UK's biggest bird of prey will threaten their livestock.

item.title: Alberta judge tosses out petition for province to separate from Canada
item.description: The ruling comes after a group leading a petition for Alberta independence said it had gathered enough signatures to trigger a province-wide vote.

item.title: WhatsApp launches totally private 'incognito' conversations with its AI chatbot
item.description: A cyber security expert says deleting chat history could lead to a lack of accountability if things go wrong.

item.title: Israel's Eurovision entry 'shocked' by protests during semi-final show
item.description: Noam Bettan was met with a mixture of cheering, boos and chants as he performed in Vienna on Tuesday night.

item.title: How worried should we be about hantavirus?
item.description: Passengers potentially exposed to hantavirus are being repatriated, so what is the risk to the wider public?

item.title: Chris Mason: Big moment ahead for Streeting to decide whether to go for it
item.description: There is a big moment coming in the next 24 hours, for Sir Keir Starmer and for would-be challengers to the prime minister.

item.title: Ethiopian woman's joy at rare quintuplets after 12 years trying for a baby
item.description: The woman, 35, says she was praying for a baby and was "overjoyed" to be "blessed with five at once".

item.title: Met Police prepares armoured vehicles and 4,000 officers for dual London protests
item.description: It comes as a Unite the Kingdom rally is taking place in central London on the same day as the annual Nakba march.

item.title: Meet the 19-metre octopus that prowled the ancient seas
item.description: Giant octopuses may have ruled the oceans 100 million years ago, according to fossil evidence.

item.title: Hearts' McInnes rages at 'disgusting' Celtic penalty
item.description: Hearts head coach Derek McInnes says his side are "up against everybody" after a "disgusting" 99th-minute penalty winner for Celtic ensured the Scottish Premiership title race goes to a final-day decider on Saturday.

item.title: Margot Robbie backs Tudor play 1536: 'Women are still having the same conversations'
item.description: Acclaimed Tudor-set play 1536 is co-produced by Margot Robbie and is being turned into a BBC TV drama.

item.title: Why big tech is betting on cute mascots
item.description: The likes of Apple, Microsoft and Google are all putting cartoon characters centre stage.

item.title: 'We're living in a shed because of river pollution'
item.description: Jane and Tony Coyle spent seven years waiting for planning permission due to River Lugg pollution.

item.title: Trump's Fed chair pick Kevin Warsh confirmed by US Senate
item.description: Kevin Warsh was confirmed by the narrowest margin since the role required a Senate confirmation vote.

item.title: Recalled Robinson one of best bowlers in the world - Key
item.description: England director of cricket Rob Key said Ollie Robinson is "one of the best bowlers in the world" after ending the Sussex seamer's two-year international exile.

item.title: 'Too dangerous to ever be free' - Utah mother who poisoned husband sentenced to life
item.description: Kouri Richins published a children's book about grief soon after her husband's death, which a jury later found she had caused.

item.title: Is this actually what Anne Boleyn looked like?
item.description: An algorithm has suggested images of what were thought to be Anne Boleyn might not be.

item.title: More than 70 million warnings sent to people seeking child abuse material
item.description: The messages highlight the illegality of what people are searching for online and direct them towards help.

item.title: Posh sandwich has more salt than nearly five cheeseburgers
item.description: Action on Salt & Sugar said people should not be exposed to a "hidden health risk every time they buy lunch".

item.title: Smart glasses are 'an invasion of privacy' - Meta's are selling better than ever
item.description: The biggest tech firms are set to sell millions of smart glasses despite growing privacy concerns.

item.title: James Van Der Beek's widow posts emotional tribute: 'Words just don't capture what grief is'
item.description: The Dawson's Creek actor died in February and his wife, Kimberly, says "the reality is settling in".

item.title: Is this the real face of Anne Boleyn?
item.description: A computer science team believes they have discovered a previously unknown sketch of King Henry VIII's second wife - but not everyone is convinced.

item.title: Streeting allies expect leadership challenge against Starmer
item.description: The health secretary's supporters tell the BBC he could launch a leadership bid as soon as Thursday.

item.title: Assist of the season? Mesmerising Foden keeps title hopes alive
item.description: Phil Foden's magical first-half assist proves the catalyst that Manchester City needed to maintain their challenge in this season's fascinating Premier League title race.

item.title: From tourist tax to digital ID: BBC correspondents on the key bills in the King's Speech
item.description: BBC correspondents look at some of the new bills Sir Keir Starmer's government wants to pass.

item.title: Farage faces standards probe into £5m gift from crypto billionaire
item.description: The probe will examine whether the Reform UK leader should have declared the gift from crypto billionaire.

item.title: Higher Europe air fares 'inevitable', says industry head
item.description: Flying by air will get more expensive as oil prices remain high after the US and Israel's conflict with Iran.

item.title: Woman isolating on British island in South Pacific after hantavirus contact
item.description: The woman who was on a virus-hit ship shows "no signs of illness", the government of Pitcairn Islands, a British Overseas Territory, says.

item.title: Lockdown in Philippine Senate after gunshots fired
item.description: Police commandos entered the building where a senator wanted by the International Criminal Court had sought refuge.

item.title: Could Southampton be kicked out of play-offs over Spygate?
item.description: Southampton will play Hull in the Championship play-off final. Or will they? As Spygate moves towards its conclusion, Middlesbrough could yet head to Wembley.

item.title: Starmer must go before next election, say Labour-backing unions
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer has vowed to get on with governing despite pressure from his MPs and cabinet splits.

item.title: Gordon agrees to Bayern move - Thursday's gossip
item.description: Bayern Munich reach Anthony Gordon agreement, Ander Barrenetxea attracting Premier League interest and Manchester United prepare Elliot Anderson move.

item.title: Not so dusty: How tech is changing woodworking
item.description: Woodworking shops have been transformed by tech to make tools safer and more precise.

item.title: First ever talks to ditch fossil fuels as UN deadlock deepens
item.description: Some 60 countries are meeting in Colombia as frustration grows with lack of progress in tackling climate change

item.title: Durham to Bedford - Gay's 200-mile drive to tell parents of England call-up
item.description: On the day he learned he would be England’s new opener, Emilio Gay drove from Durham to Bedford to tell his parents about his first international call-up.

item.title: Epstein abused me while under house arrest, survivor tells US lawmakers
item.description: Roza was among several victims who gave testimony to Democratic lawmakers on Tuesday.

item.title: Taylor named England fielding coach in landmark move
item.description: Former wicketkeeper Sarah Taylor named England's fielding coach, making her the first woman to coach an England side in a major sport.

item.title: How are countries responding to hantavirus?
item.description: The UK, US and EU are asking all citizens returning home from the virus-hit MV Hondius to self-isolate for about six weeks.

item.title: MPs call for sunbed advertising ban to help prevent skin cancer
item.description: A report by a cross-party group of MPs has found the majority of skin cancer cases are preventable.

item.title: Trade, Iran and Taiwan on the agenda as Trump arrives in China for high-stakes talks with Xi
item.description: The US president says his first request to Xi would be to "open up" China, while Beijing is expected to press Trump on the status of Taiwan.

item.title: Sydney dentist's patients warned of viruses including HIV
item.description: Authorities urged patients to get tested due to "poor infection control practices" at the Australian clinic.

item.title: UK's biggest ever environmental pollution claim reaches High Court
item.description: One of the UK's largest chicken producers and a water company accused of polluting three rivers including the River Wye

item.title: Watch: What next after the King's Speech?
item.description: BBC Chief Political Correspondent Henry Zeffman explains what is next for the government after the King's Speech.

item.title: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill 22 people, health ministry says
item.description: Eight children are among those reported dead after a series of air strikes hit the south of Beirut.

item.title: Elon Musk and Jensen Huang among CEOs joining Trump on China trip
item.description: More than a dozen US executives have joined the president on his visit, where he will meet his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping.

item.title: Princess Eugenie's charity faces further scrutiny from Charity Commission
item.description: The watchdog says it is opening a case into The Anti-Slavery Collective to assess concerns over spending.

item.title: Egg sandwiches and isolation - Life in US hantavirus quarantine
item.description: Jake Rosmarin tells the BBC what it's like inside the national quarantine unit where he and 14 other passengers are staying in Nebraska.

item.title: What is hantavirus and how does it spread?
item.description: The Andes strain of the virus, which can rarely be passed from person to person, has been found among passengers of a cruise ship.

item.title: Dozens of dogs rescued and suspect arrested in Uganda after BBC investigation
item.description: A BBC investigation exposed how scammers posted social content of dogs in distress to dupe donors into giving money.

item.title: Global forest loss slows but El Niño fires could threaten progress
item.description: The loss of tropical rainforests eased last year, according to new analysis, but scientists warn they are still disappearing rapidly.

item.title: Court overturns Alex Murdaugh's murder convictions and orders new trial
item.description: The court has ordered a new trial over the June 2021 killings of Paul and Maggie Murdaugh.

item.title: Paul Chuckle to make National Theatre debut
item.description: The children's entertainer, 78, will play Mr Boo in The Rise and Fall of Little Voice.

item.title: Eurovision boss: 'We're watching the voting very carefully'
item.description: Martin Green says attempts to influence voters will be taken seriously, but stops short of sanctions.

item.title: Bodies of three young women pulled from sea off Brighton
item.description: Police leave the scene and Brighton beach reopens after the bodies of three women are found.

item.title: Texas accuses Netflix of spying on users, including children
item.description: The lawsuit comes amid increased scrutiny over platform features like auto-play that deliver endless content to users.

item.title: US drops $15,000 visa deposit for foreign fans with World Cup tickets
item.description: Fifty countries, including five that have qualified for the World Cup, are subject to the bond requirement as part of Trump's crackdown on visa overstays.

item.title: Reform councillor 'brought party into disrepute'
item.description: Reform UK say Sheffield councillor Nathaniel Menday has been suspended pending an investigation.

item.title: Rhun ap Iorwerth reveals ministerial team, promising 'new era' for Wales
item.description: Ap Iorwerth said it was not just a change in administration but a change of approach for governing Wales.

item.title: Shrinking Milka chocolate bar tricked consumers, says German court
item.description: A court in Bremen has found the manufacturer of the classic Alpine Milk chocolate bar guilty of "shrinkflation".

item.title: Elon Musk said control of OpenAI should go to his children, Sam Altman tells jury
item.description: Sam Altman said Elon Musk tried many times for total control of OpenAI, which he's now suing.

item.title: The potential challengers to Keir Starmer
item.description: Andy Burnham, Angela Rayner and Wes Streeting are all potential candidates for the top job.

item.title: Starmer warns against leadership contest in pleas to ministers and MPs
item.description: Sir Keir battles to save his job, as Health Secretary Wes Streeting is thought to be plotting a leadership challenge potentially as early as Thursday.

item.title: Does England's defence put winning streak at risk?
item.description: Does England's leaky defence put their record winning streak at risk in Sunday's Grand Slam decider against France in Bordeaux?

item.title: An iron fist joining a broken team: Inside Mourinho's Real return
item.description: Jose Mourinho is set to be appointed Real Madrid's new manager. Spanish football expert Guillem Balague explains why.

item.title: Suicide forum fined £950,000 for not blocking UK users
item.description: The regulator said the forum had not done enough to protect UK users - but critics accuse Ofcom of acting too slowly.

item.title: Ex-rugby star on the joys of his own burger stall
item.description: Ex-England and Leicester Tigers hooker Tom Youngs says the new venture brings families to the farm.

item.title: Daily pill to help keep weight off after stopping obesity jabs
item.description: The tablet - orforglipron - is available in the US and could soon launch in the UK.

item.title: Canvas hack: Company pays criminals to delete students' stolen data
item.description: The company behind Canvas says it has "reached an agreement" with the hackers who disrupted thousands of colleges and universities.

item.title: 'Bingo! It's a match'- Franklin expedition sailor's DNA links to BBC reporter
item.description: Researchers at the University of Waterloo found remains of Rich Preston's great great great uncle John Bridgens.

item.title: Why Arsenal have fallen short in WSL and Champions League this season
item.description: Why have Renee Slegers' side not been able to get their hands on any of the season's biggest prizes?

item.title: Deadly Russian drone attacks on Ukraine resume after ceasefire expires
item.description: Six people have been killed after Zelensky warned of "more waves" of Russian strikes through Wednesday.

item.title: Tui sees summer sales fall 10% due to cautious UK customers
item.description: The travel operator says customers are delaying booking holidays over Iran war concerns.

item.title: £20m mystery gift buys London Zoo new hospital where you can watch vets work
item.description: Visitors will be able to watch live veterinary procedures inside a state-of-the-art new animal hospital.

item.title: Olly Murs pushed to his limits by charity challenge
item.description: The 41-year-old is running, cycling and rowing between Manchester and London.

item.title: Contact with pair held in Iran cut off, family says
item.description: Lindsay and Craig Foreman, from East Sussex, have been detained in Iran for more than a year.

item.title: What to know as Trump visits Xi in China
item.description: Trump has landed in Beijing for the first visit by a US leader since his own in 2017.

item.title: Thousands of Waymos recalled after robotaxi swept into a creek
item.description: The voluntary recall follows an incident on 20 April where an empty Waymo car entered a flooded road in San Antonio, Texas.

item.title: Thief jailed after stealing unreleased Beyoncé music from car
item.description: The man smashed into a car and stole luggage containing hard drives with the singer's tracks.

item.title: Massive Alaska megatsunami was second largest ever recorded
item.description: New research suggests glacier melt driven by climate change is increasing the risk of giant waves.
